title: dombeya ameliae var. louvelii
intro: Dombeya ameliae var. louvelii, also known as the 'Rose Bush of Madagascar', is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving digestive issues. In Madagascar, the plant is used to prepare herbal remedies, such as tea and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments.
